column1: #mapping_set_description: "Cross-ontology mappings between the Personal Agent Ontology governance module and the W3C Data Privacy Vocabulary (DPV) for privacy compliance interoperability."

column8: Both represent governance rules. PAO PermissionPolicy is agent-centric (tool permissions); DPV Policy covers broader data protection policies.
PAO SafetyConstraint is a behavioral limit; DPV TOM is a broader category of safeguards. Structural parallel in governing agent behavior.
Both represent deliberate data deletion for privacy compliance.
An AI agent may act as data controller; PAO Agent is broader. Context-dependent mapping.
In agent-user interactions, the human user is typically the data subject whose personal data is processed.
PAO grantsPermission links policies to allowed actions; DPV hasLegalBasis links processing to legal grounds. Functional parallel.
PAO MemoryItem may contain personal data; DPV PersonalData is specifically data relating to an identified person. Not all MemoryItems are PersonalData.
Both annotate data with sensitivity classification.
